The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India violently exploded back into the massive national consciousness. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 20, 1945, the incredibly massive, historically unprecedented, and absolutely definitive Nuremberg Trials officially and aggressively commenced in Germany. The International Military Tribunal, heavily established by the massive Allied powers, aggressively prosecuted 24 of the absolute most massive, surviving political and military leaders of Nazi Germany. This incredibly massive, highly televised judicial process fundamentally established the absolute, foundational legal architecture of modern international human rights law, firmly declaring that aggressive wars and massive crimes against humanity would be fiercely prosecuted globally.

On November 20, 1985, the incredibly massive, highly successful, and universally renowned technological revolution known as Microsoft Windows 1.0 was officially and aggressively released. This incredibly massive operating environment heavily introduced an absolute, highly intuitive graphical user interface (GUI) to IBM-compatible personal computers. While initially heavily mocked, this incredibly massive technological launch fundamentally and aggressively set the absolute stage for Microsoft's massive, multi-decade monopoly over the entire massive global desktop computing architecture.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 20, 1750, Tipu Sultan, the incredibly massive, fiercely formidable, and absolutely brilliant "Tiger of Mysore," was officially born in Devanahalli. He achieved absolute, historical immortality by heavily reforming his massive military with advanced rocket technology and fiercely leading an absolute, incredibly aggressive series of massive Anglo-Mysore Wars. His massive, uncompromising resistance against the aggressive expansion of the British East India Company firmly cemented his legacy as a massive, towering symbol of Indian anti-colonial defiance.

On November 20, 1910, B. D. Jatti, the massive, highly respected Indian politician and fiercely dedicated trade unionist, was officially born in Karnataka. He achieved absolute, historical immortality by serving as the 5th Vice President of India, and heavily acting as the incredibly massive, Acting President of India in 1977 following the tragic death of Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed. His incredibly massive, highly dignified tenure aggressively stabilized the incredibly volatile, massive constitutional transition leading to the incredibly historic 1977 general elections.
